mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines below 2.5% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.19% suggests easing environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk, causing equity investors to reduce risk exposure and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as sentiment shifts.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.0% and increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.7% is below trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns becomes insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with housing starts (HOUST) expansion signals robust consumer demand and construction activity, indicating strong economic momentum that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS grows month-over-month and HOUST exceeds 1200 thousand units simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 511488M and HOUST at 1201K both near trigger levels
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with sustained demand growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where both conditions are met, or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either component alone.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction below 108.0 signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk, causing equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines below 108.0 and falls more than 0.3 points month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 108.48 is above trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE below 4.0%) signals tight labor market conditions that increase wage pressure and inflation expectations, causing the Fed to maintain higher rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS grows month-over-month by more than 100K and UNRATE remains below 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 149750K and UNRATE at 3.7% both satisfy trigger conditions
regime_hint: tight labor market with inflation concerns
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal produces lower predictive power than UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) rising above 255.0 combined with elevated federal funds rate above 2.5% signals persistent monetary tightness that constrains equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as real rates remain restrictive.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds 255.0 and FEDFUNDS remains above 2.5% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 252.83 is near trigger, FEDFUNDS at 2.19% is below
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows lower Rank IC than either component measured independently.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at levels above 18700 billion dollars signals sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 exceeds 18700 billion chained 2017 dollars and quarter-over-quarter growth is positive
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 18671.50 is near trigger threshold
regime_hint: economic expansion with positive growth momentum
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) expansion signals broad-based supply and labor capacity increases that support productivity gains and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO grows month-over-month and PAYEMS increases by more than 50K in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 108.48 and PAYEMS at 149750K suggest capacity expansion
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal produces lower Rank IC than INDPRO alone over any 2-year rolling window.
